bead.title: Visibility 5: ddx agent check [--provider X] — runtime availability
bead.description:
Add a new DDx CLI subcommand `ddx agent check [--provider X]`. Semantic distinction from existing `ddx agent doctor`: doctor answers "is my config failed?" (config validation, malformed YAML, missing API keys); check answers "what agents can I use right now?" (runtime liveness, which providers respond, which models are currently available). Both commands coexist.

Implementation lives in cli/cmd/agent_check.go. Mirrors ddx-agent's `cmdCheck` — probes each configured provider's /v1/models endpoint, reports reachability and current model inventory, exits 0 if at least one provider is reachable and has at least one usable model, exits 1 otherwise. Uses the Go package, not shell-out.
bead.acceptance: (1) `ddx agent check` reports runtime liveness for every configured provider, not config sanity. (2) `ddx agent check --provider vidar` probes only vidar and reports result. (3) `ddx agent doctor` still exists and is about config/registry health, NOT provider liveness (semantic separation preserved). (4) Exit code 0 when at least one provider is reachable and has at least one model; 1 otherwise. (5) `go build ./...` passes. (6) Manual: stop lmstudio on vidar temporarily, run `ddx agent check --provider vidar`, see explicit unreachable status; restart, run again, see connected + model count.
